MAJOR","groupId":"be157945-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":0,"area":"Requirements"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Food Science is a discipline in which biological, physical, and sensory sciences are integrated for the study of foods to ensure their safety, quality, and healthful properties. The food science curriculum encompasses food chemistry and biochemistry, food safety and microbiology, food processing and preservation, and sensory and consumer sciences.


.Food Science is a multidisciplinary major that includes core competencies in food chemistry and analysis, food safety and microbiology, and food processing and engineering. After completing the preparatory course work, students focus on advanced subject courses in food chemistry and biochemistry, food processing, sensory evaluation, and food microbiology applied to improve the safety, stability, taste, nutrition, convenience, sustainability and value of foods.

Opportunities for employment include positions in the food and allied industries, government agencies, and educational and research institutions. Graduate study for the food science student may lead to the M.S. or Ph.D. degree in food science, or in related fields such as agricultural chemistry, biochemistry, microbiology, nutrition and health sciences.

At the time of TAG submission, this major requires a minimum 3.20 cumulative GPA, with no grade lower than a C to receive a Transfer Admission Guarantee.

","groupId":"67a16f45-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":7,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

This major is not currently screening for major preparation at the time of admission; however, it is strongly encouraged that you complete as many major preparation courses as possible prior to transfer. Incomplete foundational coursework may delay your ability to graduate on time.

 

Visit Department agreements as needed


Contact your UC Davis Advisor for questions (Find my advisor)


Note:

Advanced Placement (AP) and International Baccalaureate (IB) Examination:

AP and IB examination credit policies are detailed in the UC Davis General Catalog. Quick reference charts for AP and IB.
